notes: Chapter is overwhelmingly theological/demonological in content, built around a chain of Scripture quotations (marked with footnote numbers 897-913 in source, not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this excerpt). Attributed_quote claims are flagged needs_verification:true for quote-fidelity checking against the underlying Scripture text and against the footnote apparatus of the 1911 Appendix; their claim_text offers a best-effort common-knowledge scripture identification for the verifier's convenience but this is not authoritative. Two claims (statistic on Roman legion size, biographical claim about Simon Magus/Elymas/the Philippian slave girl) are genuinely checkable against historical/biblical-critical scholarship independent of theology. Two sweeping sociological/interpretive claims about contemporary (c. 1911) religious opinion are logged as interpretive per pipeline rules.

claims.quote: There is a growing tendency to disbelief in the existence of evil spirits, while the holy angels that “minister for them who shall be heirs of salvation,”(897) are regarded by many as the spirits of the dead.
claims.claim_text: By the time of writing (early 20th century), there was a growing societal tendency to disbelieve in the existence of evil spirits, while many people regarded angels as the spirits of deceased human beings.
claims.extractor_note: Sweeping sociological/interpretive claim about contemporary religious opinion (plausibly referencing 19th-century Spiritualism); logged as interpretive per pipeline rules, not for fact-checking.

claims.quote: the holy angels that “minister for them who shall be heirs of salvation,”(897)
claims.claim_text: Scripture (per the footnote marker 897, commonly identified with Hebrews 1:14) describes angels as those who 'minister for them who shall be heirs of salvation.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (897) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: But the Scriptures not only teach the existence of angels, both good and evil, but present unquestionable proof that these are not the disembodied spirits of dead men.
claims.claim_text: The Scriptures teach the existence of both good and evil angels and provide proof that these angels are not the disembodied spirits of dead people.

claims.quote: Before the creation of man, angels were in existence; for when the foundations of the earth were laid, “the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy.”(898)
claims.claim_text: Angels existed before the creation of man, as evidenced by angels singing and shouting for joy when the foundations of the earth were laid.

claims.quote: “the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy.”(898)
claims.claim_text: Scripture (footnote 898, commonly identified with Job 38:7) states that at the laying of the earth's foundations 'the morning stars sang together, and all the sons of God shouted for joy.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (898) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: After the fall of man, angels were sent to guard the tree of life, and this before a human being had died.
claims.claim_text: After the fall of man, angels were sent to guard the tree of life, and this occurred before any human being had died.

claims.quote: Angels are in nature superior to men; for the psalmist says that man was made “a little lower than the angels.”(899)
claims.claim_text: The Psalms (footnote 899, commonly identified with Psalm 8:5) state that man was made 'a little lower than the angels,' indicating angels are by nature superior to men.
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (899) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: “The Lord hath prepared His throne in the heavens; and His kingdom ruleth over all.”
claims.claim_text: Scripture (commonly identified with Psalm 103:19) states 'The Lord hath prepared His throne in the heavens; and His kingdom ruleth over all.'
claims.extractor_note: No footnote marker attached in source to this specific quote; verifier should confirm exact citation.

claims.quote: And, says the prophet, “I heard the voice of many angels round about the throne.”
claims.claim_text: A biblical prophet (commonly identified as John in Revelation 5:11) is quoted as saying 'I heard the voice of many angels round about the throne.'

claims.quote: In the presence-chamber of the King of kings they wait—“angels, that excel in strength,” “ministers of His, that do His pleasure,” “hearkening unto the voice of His word.”(900)
claims.claim_text: Scripture (footnote 900, commonly identified with Psalm 103:20) describes angels in God's presence-chamber as those who 'excel in strength,' are 'ministers of His, that do His pleasure,' and are 'hearkening unto the voice of His word.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (900) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: Ten thousand times ten thousand and thousands of thousands, were the heavenly messengers beheld by the prophet Daniel.
claims.claim_text: The prophet Daniel is recorded as having beheld 'ten thousand times ten thousand and thousands of thousands' heavenly messengers (commonly identified with Daniel 7:10).

claims.quote: The apostle Paul declared them “an innumerable company.”(901)
claims.claim_text: The apostle Paul is quoted (footnote 901, commonly identified with Hebrews 12:22) as describing angels as 'an innumerable company.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (901) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: As God’s messengers they go forth, like “the appearance of a flash of lightning,”(902) so dazzling their glory, and so swift their flight.
claims.claim_text: Scripture (footnote 902, commonly identified with Ezekiel 1:14) describes angels going forth 'like the appearance of a flash of lightning.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (902) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: The angel that appeared at the Saviour’s tomb, his countenance “like lightning, and his raiment white as snow,” caused the keepers for fear of him to quake, and they “became as dead men.”(903)
claims.claim_text: An angel appeared at Jesus's tomb with a countenance 'like lightning' and raiment 'white as snow', causing the guards to quake with fear and 'become as dead men' (commonly identified with Matthew 28:2-4).
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (903) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: When Sennacherib, the haughty Assyrian, reproached and blasphemed God, and threatened Israel with destruction, “it came to pass that night, that the angel of the Lord went out, and smote in the camp of the Assyrians an hundred fourscore and five thousand.”
claims.claim_text: According to the biblical account (2 Kings 19 / Isaiah 37), after Sennacherib of Assyria reproached and blasphemed God and threatened Israel with destruction, the angel of the Lord killed 185,000 men in the camp of the Assyrians in a single night.

claims.quote: There were “cut off all the mighty men of valor, and the leaders and captains,” from the army of Sennacherib.
claims.claim_text: According to the biblical account, all the mighty men of valor, leaders, and captains were cut off from the army of Sennacherib of Assyria.

claims.quote: “So he returned with shame of face to his own land.”(904)
claims.claim_text: According to the biblical account (footnote 904, commonly identified with 2 Chronicles 32:21), Sennacherib returned in shame to his own land following the destruction of his army.
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (904) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: Angels are sent on missions of mercy to the children of God. To Abraham, with promises of blessing; to the gates of Sodom, to rescue righteous Lot from its fiery doom; to Elijah, as he was about to perish from weariness and hunger in the desert; to Elisha, with chariots and horses of fire surrounding the little town where he was shut in by his foes; to Daniel, while seeking divine wisdom in the court of a heathen king, or abandoned to become the lions’ prey; to Peter, doomed to death in Herod’s dungeon; to the prisoners at Philippi; to Paul and his companions in the night of tempest on the sea; to open the mind of Cornelius to receive the gospel; to dispatch Peter with the message of salvation to the Gentile stranger,—thus holy angels have, in all ages, ministered to God’s people.
claims.claim_text: According to biblical narrative, holy angels have ministered to God's people throughout history in specific episodes involving Abraham, Lot at Sodom, Elijah in the desert, Elisha at a town besieged by enemies, Daniel in a heathen king's court and in the lions' den, Peter in Herod's dungeon, prisoners at Philippi, Paul during a sea storm, Cornelius, and Peter's mission to Cornelius.

claims.quote: A guardian angel is appointed to every follower of Christ.
claims.claim_text: A guardian angel is appointed to every follower of Christ.

claims.quote: These heavenly watchers shield the righteous from the power of the wicked one.
claims.claim_text: Guardian angels shield righteous people from the power of Satan.

claims.quote: This Satan himself recognized when he said, “Doth Job fear God for naught? Hast not Thou made a hedge about him, and about his house, and about all that he hath on every side?”(905)
claims.claim_text: Satan is quoted in Scripture (footnote 905, commonly identified with Job 1:9-10) as saying of Job, 'Doth Job fear God for naught? Hast not Thou made a hedge about him, and about his house, and about all that he hath on every side?'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (905) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: The agency by which God protects His people is presented in the words of the psalmist, “The angel of the Lord encampeth round about them that fear Him, and delivereth them.”(906)
claims.claim_text: The Psalms (footnote 906, commonly identified with Psalm 34:7) state 'The angel of the Lord encampeth round about them that fear Him, and delivereth them.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (906) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: Said the Saviour, speaking of those that believe in Him, “Take heed that ye despise not one of these little ones; for I say unto you, That in heaven their angels do always behold the face of My Father.”(907)
claims.claim_text: Jesus is quoted (footnote 907, commonly identified with Matthew 18:10) as saying of believers, 'Take heed that ye despise not one of these little ones; for I say unto you, That in heaven their angels do always behold the face of My Father.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (907) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: The angels appointed to minister to the children of God have at all times access to His presence.
claims.claim_text: Angels appointed to minister to God's children have at all times access to God's presence.

claims.quote: Thus God’s people, exposed to the deceptive power and unsleeping malice of the prince of darkness, and in conflict with all the forces of evil, are assured of the unceasing guardianship of heavenly angels.
claims.claim_text: God's people, though exposed to the deceptive power and unsleeping malice of Satan and in conflict with all forces of evil, are assured of the unceasing guardianship of heavenly angels.

claims.quote: If God has granted to His children promise of grace and protection, it is because there are mighty agencies of evil to be met,—agencies numerous, determined, and untiring, of whose malignity and power none can safely be ignorant or unheeding.
claims.claim_text: God's promise of grace and protection to His children exists because there are numerous, determined, and untiring evil agencies to be met, whose malignity and power no one can safely ignore.

claims.quote: Evil spirits, in the beginning created sinless, were equal in nature, power, and glory with the holy beings that are now God’s messengers.
claims.claim_text: Evil spirits were originally created sinless and equal in nature, power, and glory to the holy angels that are now God's messengers.

claims.quote: United with Satan in his rebellion, and with him cast out from heaven, they have, through all succeeding ages, co-operated with him in his warfare against the divine authority.
claims.claim_text: Evil spirits united with Satan in his rebellion, were cast out of heaven with him, and have cooperated with him in warfare against divine authority throughout all subsequent ages.

claims.quote: Old Testament history presents occasional mentions of their existence and agency; but it was during the time when Christ was upon the earth that evil spirits manifested their power in the most striking manner.
claims.claim_text: Old Testament history contains only occasional mentions of evil spirits' existence and activity, whereas evil spirits manifested their power most strikingly during the time Christ was on earth.

claims.quote: He had succeeded in establishing idolatry in every part of the earth except the land of Palestine.
claims.claim_text: By the time of Christ, Satan had succeeded in establishing idolatry in every part of the earth except the land of Palestine.
claims.extractor_note: Causal claim attributing global idolatry to Satan's agency, framed theologically; also contains an underlying historical premise (near-universal idolatry outside Palestine in antiquity) that a reader might separately fact-check, but the claim as stated is supernatural-causal and logged as theological per pipeline rules.

claims.quote: Satan raged like a chained lion, and defiantly exhibited his power over the bodies as well as the souls of men.
claims.claim_text: During Christ's earthly ministry, Satan raged like a chained lion and defiantly exhibited his power over both the bodies and souls of men.

claims.quote: The fact that men have been possessed with demons, is clearly stated in the New Testament.
claims.claim_text: The New Testament clearly states that men have been possessed with demons.

claims.quote: The persons thus afflicted were not merely suffering with disease from natural causes.
claims.claim_text: Persons afflicted with demon possession, as recorded in the New Testament, were not merely suffering from disease due to natural causes.

claims.quote: Christ had perfect understanding of that with which He was dealing, and He recognized the direct presence and agency of evil spirits.
claims.claim_text: Christ had perfect understanding of demon possession and recognized the direct presence and agency of evil spirits in those he treated.

claims.quote: A striking example of their number, power, and malignity, and also of the power and mercy of Christ, is given in the Scripture account of the healing of the demoniacs at Gadara.
claims.claim_text: The Scripture account of the healing of the demoniacs at Gadara is a striking example of the number, power, and malignity of demons, and also of the power and mercy of Christ.

claims.quote: One of the demons controlling the sufferers declared, “My name is Legion: for we are many.”(908)
claims.claim_text: A demon controlling the Gadarene demoniacs is quoted in Scripture (footnote 908, commonly identified with Mark 5:9 / Luke 8:30) as declaring 'My name is Legion: for we are many.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (908) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: In the Roman army a legion consisted of from three to five thousand men.
claims.claim_text: In the Roman army, a legion consisted of from three thousand to five thousand men.
claims.extractor_note: Genuinely independent historical/military claim about Roman legion strength, checkable against classical military history scholarship regardless of the surrounding theological framing.

claims.quote: Satan’s hosts also are marshaled in companies, and the single company to which these demons belonged numbered no less than a legion.
claims.claim_text: Satan's hosts of evil spirits are organized into companies, and the specific company of demons possessing the Gadarene demoniacs numbered no less than a Roman legion (three to five thousand).

claims.quote: At the command of Jesus, the evil spirits departed from their victims, leaving them calmly sitting at the Saviour’s feet, subdued, intelligent, and gentle.
claims.claim_text: At Jesus's command, the evil spirits departed from the Gadarene demoniacs, leaving them calmly sitting at Jesus's feet, subdued, intelligent, and gentle.

claims.quote: But the demons were permitted to sweep a herd of swine into the sea; and to the dwellers of Gadara the loss of these outweighed the blessings which Christ had bestowed, and the divine Healer was entreated to depart.
claims.claim_text: The demons cast out of the Gadarene demoniacs were permitted to sweep a herd of swine into the sea, and the residents of Gadara, valuing the loss of the swine over the healing Christ had performed, asked Jesus to leave.

claims.quote: This was the result which Satan designed to secure. By casting the blame of their loss upon Jesus, he aroused the selfish fears of the people, and prevented them from listening to His words.
claims.claim_text: Satan designed the outcome at Gadara so that, by casting blame for the loss of the swine on Jesus, he would arouse the people's selfish fears and prevent them from listening to Jesus's words.

claims.quote: Satan is constantly accusing Christians as the cause of loss, misfortune, and suffering, instead of allowing the reproach to fall where it belongs,—upon himself and his agents.
claims.claim_text: Satan constantly accuses Christians as the cause of loss, misfortune, and suffering, rather than allowing the reproach to fall on himself and his agents where it belongs.

claims.quote: He allowed the evil spirits to destroy the herd of swine as a rebuke to those Jews who were raising these unclean beasts for the sake of gain.
claims.claim_text: Christ allowed the evil spirits to destroy the herd of swine at Gadara as a rebuke to the Jews who were raising swine, an unclean animal under Jewish law, for financial gain.

claims.quote: Had not Christ restrained the demons, they would have plunged into the sea, not only the swine, but also their keepers and owners.
claims.claim_text: Had Christ not restrained the demons at Gadara, the demons would have plunged into the sea not only the swine but also their keepers and owners.

claims.quote: The preservation of both the keepers and the owners was due alone to His power, mercifully exercised for their deliverance.
claims.claim_text: The preservation of the swine's keepers and owners at Gadara was due solely to Christ's power, mercifully exercised for their deliverance.

claims.quote: Furthermore, this event was permitted to take place that the disciples might witness the cruel power of Satan upon both man and beast.
claims.claim_text: The Gadarene swine incident was permitted to occur so that Christ's disciples might witness Satan's cruel power over both man and beast.

claims.quote: The Saviour desired His followers to have a knowledge of the foe whom they were to meet, that they might not be deceived and overcome by his devices.
claims.claim_text: Jesus desired his followers to have knowledge of Satan as the foe they were to meet, so that they would not be deceived and overcome by his devices.

claims.quote: It was also His will that the people of that region should behold His power to break the bondage of Satan and release his captives.
claims.claim_text: It was Christ's will that the people of the Gadara region should witness his power to break Satan's bondage and release his captives.

claims.quote: And though Jesus Himself departed, the men so marvelously delivered, remained to declare the mercy of their Benefactor.
claims.claim_text: Although Jesus departed from the Gadara region after being asked to leave, the men who had been delivered from demon possession remained to declare the mercy of Christ, their benefactor.

claims.quote: The daughter of the Syro-Phenician woman was grievously vexed with a devil, whom Jesus cast out by His word.(909)
claims.claim_text: According to the New Testament (footnote 909, commonly identified with Matthew 15:21-28 / Mark 7:24-30), the daughter of a Syro-Phoenician woman was grievously afflicted by a demon, which Jesus cast out by his word.
claims.extractor_note: Narrates a specific named biblical episode involving demon possession; kept theological per pipeline rules on supernatural claims, though it also names a specific (unnamed-by-name but identifiable) biblical figure.

claims.quote: One “possessed with a devil, blind, and dumb;”(910)
claims.claim_text: The New Testament (footnote 910, commonly identified with Matthew 12:22) records a person 'possessed with a devil, blind, and dumb' who was healed by Jesus.
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (910) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: a youth who had a dumb spirit, that ofttimes “cast him into the fire, and into the waters, to destroy him;”(911)
claims.claim_text: The New Testament (footnote 911, commonly identified with Mark 9:22) records a youth possessed by a 'dumb spirit' that often cast him into fire and water to destroy him, who was healed by Jesus.
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (911) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: the maniac who, tormented by “a spirit of an unclean devil,”(912) disturbed the Sabbath quiet of the synagogue at Capernaum,—all were healed by the compassionate Saviour.
claims.claim_text: The New Testament (footnote 912, commonly identified with Luke 4:33) records a maniac tormented by 'a spirit of an unclean devil' who disturbed the Sabbath quiet of the synagogue at Capernaum, and who was healed by Jesus.
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (912) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: In nearly every instance, Christ addressed the demon as an intelligent entity, commanding him to come out of his victim and to torment him no more.
claims.claim_text: In nearly every recorded instance of exorcism, Christ addressed the demon as an intelligent entity, commanding it to come out of its victim and torment him no more.

claims.quote: The worshipers at Capernaum, beholding His mighty power, “were all amazed, and spake among themselves, saying, ‘What a word is this! for with authority and power He commandeth the unclean spirits, and they come out.’ ”(913)
claims.claim_text: The New Testament (footnote 913, commonly identified with Luke 4:36) records that the worshipers at Capernaum, upon beholding Christ's power to cast out unclean spirits, were amazed and said among themselves, 'What a word is this! for with authority and power He commandeth the unclean spirits, and they come out.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (913) not resolved to book/chapter/verse in this chapter file; verifier should confirm exact citation via the 1911 Appendix.

claims.quote: Those possessed with devils are usually represented as being in a condition of great suffering; yet there were exceptions to this rule.
claims.claim_text: Those possessed with demons in Scripture are usually represented as being in a condition of great suffering, though there were exceptions to this pattern.

claims.quote: For the sake of obtaining supernatural power, some welcomed the satanic influence. These of course had no conflict with the demons.
claims.claim_text: Some individuals welcomed satanic influence for the sake of obtaining supernatural power and therefore had no conflict with the demons possessing them.

claims.quote: Of this class were those who possessed the spirit of divination,—Simon Magus, Elymas the sorcerer, and the damsel who followed Paul and Silas at Philippi.
claims.claim_text: Simon Magus, Elymas the sorcerer, and the slave girl who followed Paul and Silas at Philippi are biblical figures identified as having possessed a 'spirit of divination' and welcomed rather than resisted this influence.

claims.quote: None are in greater danger from the influence of evil spirits than those who, notwithstanding the direct and ample testimony of the Scriptures, deny the existence and agency of the devil and his angels.
claims.claim_text: Those who deny the existence and agency of the devil and his angels, despite the direct and ample testimony of Scripture, are in greater danger from the influence of evil spirits than anyone else.

claims.quote: So long as we are ignorant of their wiles, they have almost inconceivable advantage; many give heed to their suggestions while they suppose themselves to be following the dictates of their own wisdom.
claims.claim_text: As long as people remain ignorant of the wiles of evil spirits, the spirits have an almost inconceivable advantage, and many people follow the spirits' suggestions while believing they are following their own wisdom.

claims.quote: This is why, as we approach the close of time, when Satan is to work with greatest power to deceive and destroy, he spreads everywhere the belief that he does not exist.
claims.claim_text: As the end of time approaches and Satan is to work with the greatest power to deceive and destroy, Satan spreads everywhere the belief that he does not exist.

claims.quote: It is his policy to conceal himself and his manner of working.
claims.claim_text: It is Satan's policy to conceal himself and his manner of working.

claims.quote: There is nothing that the great deceiver fears so much as that we shall become acquainted with his devices.
claims.claim_text: Satan, the great deceiver, fears nothing so much as that people should become acquainted with his devices.

claims.quote: The better to disguise his real character and purposes, he has caused himself to be so represented as to excite no stronger emotion than ridicule or contempt. He is well pleased to be painted as a ludicrous or loathsome object, misshapen, half animal and half human.
claims.claim_text: Satan, to disguise his real character and purposes, has caused himself to be represented in ways that excite no stronger emotion than ridicule or contempt, and is pleased to be depicted as a ludicrous or loathsome object, misshapen, half animal and half human.

claims.quote: He is pleased to hear his name used in sport and mockery by those who think themselves intelligent and well informed.
claims.claim_text: Satan is pleased to hear his name used in sport and mockery by people who consider themselves intelligent and well informed.

claims.quote: It is because he has masked himself with consummate skill that the question is so widely asked, “Does such a being really exist?”
claims.claim_text: Because Satan has masked himself with consummate skill, the question 'Does such a being really exist?' is widely asked.

claims.quote: It is an evidence of his success that theories giving the lie to the plainest testimony of the Scriptures are so generally received in the religious world.
claims.claim_text: By the time of writing, theories contradicting the plainest testimony of Scripture regarding Satan's existence were generally received in the religious world, which the text interprets as evidence of Satan's success.
claims.extractor_note: Sweeping sociological/interpretive claim about the state of religious opinion c. 1911; logged as interpretive per pipeline rules, not for fact-checking.

claims.quote: And it is because Satan can most readily control the minds of those who are unconscious of his influence, that the word of God gives us so many examples of his malignant work, unveiling before us his secret forces, and thus placing us on our guard against his assaults.
claims.claim_text: Because Satan can most readily control the minds of those unconscious of his influence, the word of God gives many examples of his malignant work in order to unveil his secret forces and place people on guard against his assaults.

claims.quote: The power and malice of Satan and his host might justly alarm us, were it not that we may find shelter and deliverance in the superior power of our Redeemer.
claims.claim_text: The power and malice of Satan and his host would justly alarm believers, were it not for shelter and deliverance available in the superior power of Christ the Redeemer.

claims.quote: If permitted, they can distract our minds, disorder and torment our bodies, destroy our possessions and our lives.
claims.claim_text: Evil angels, if permitted, can distract human minds, disorder and torment human bodies, and destroy human possessions and lives.

claims.quote: Fearful is the condition of those who resist the divine claims, and yield to Satan’s temptations, until God gives them up to the control of evil spirits.
claims.claim_text: The condition of those who resist divine claims and yield to Satan's temptations is fearful, since God eventually gives them up to the control of evil spirits.

claims.quote: But those who follow Christ are ever safe under His watchcare. Angels that excel in strength are sent from heaven to protect them. The wicked one cannot break through the guard which God has stationed about His people.
claims.claim_text: Those who follow Christ are always safe under his watchcare; angels that excel in strength are sent from heaven to protect them, and Satan cannot break through the guard God has stationed around his people.
